title: fix: remove legacy app Worker routes
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Deletes exact old-account Worker routes for an app public hostname during cutover.
- Keeps cleanup scoped to <app>.pollinations.ai and <app>.pollinations.ai/*.
- Lets websim.pollinations.ai move from the legacy Worker route to pollinations-proxy.

Checks:
- npx biome check --write apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js
- git diff --check

gist.headline: Clearing the old flight paths
gist.blurb: We've updated our deployment scripts to automatically clean up legacy Worker routes during app cutovers. Smoother migrations for the hive!
gist.summary: Updated the app deployment script to automatically delete legacy Worker routes for <app>.pollinations.ai hostnames during cutover.
gist.impact: This allows apps like websim to seamlessly migrate from legacy Worker routes to the new pollinations-proxy without routing conflicts.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot cleaning up old glowing pathways or wires in a server room, making way for a shiny new router/proxy box.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny hard hat, working with a chunky monitor-head robot to unplug old glowing cables from a retro server rack. A shiny new router box sits nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
